findings.description: The order_id parameter is interpolated directly into a SQL query via string concatenation on line 47. An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to read, modify, or delete data in the orders table.

steps: Send POST /api/orders with a valid auth token
Set order_id to: 1; DROP TABLE orders;--
Observe 500 Internal Server Error with database error in response body
reproduction.command: curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/orders -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOi...' -d '{"order_id": "1; DROP TABLE orders;--"}'
reproduction.expected: 400 Bad Request or parameterized query escapes the input
reproduction.actual: 500 Internal Server Error — database error surfaced in response body

server_logs.message: QueryFailedError: syntax error at or near "Drop"

findings.recommended_fix: Refactor to use knex query builder instead of raw SQL, matching the convention in src/routes/users.js.
rules_violated: No raw SQL queries — must use parameterized statements

findings.description: The admin users endpoint has no authentication middleware. Any unauthenticated user can access the full user list including emails and roles.

steps: Send GET /api/admin/users with no Authorization header
Observe 200 OK with full user list

findings.recommended_fix: Add authMiddleware to the route definition and add admin role check.
rules_violated: All API endpoints must require authentication

clean_endpoints.notes: quantity is cast to integer before use
